# Multi-Workset Diff

## What
Extend `wikihistory diff` to accept N wiki URLs (not just 2), producing an N-way comparison table instead of a pairwise diff.

## Why
CW-01 compares 2 wikis. But fandom topics often exist across 3+ competing instances: Wikipedia, Fandom.com, an independent fork, and a Fandom alternative like Wiki.gg. N-way diff surfaces which wikis track together and which diverge — without running N*(N-1)/2 pairwise commands.

## Context
Read first:
- `packages/cli/src/commands/diff.ts` — existing pairwise diff implementation
- `packages/cli/src/index.ts` — CLI dispatch, `parseFlag`

## Implementation
1. Add `--wiki-*` as repeatable flag: `--wiki-a <url> --wiki-b <url> --wiki-c <url>`
2. Run full analyze pipeline independently for each wiki
3. Build an N×M matrix: rows = event types, columns = wikis, cells = count
4. Output as a table (current format) plus optional CSV export
5. Flag wikis that are statistical outliers per event type (z-score > 2)

## Invariants
- CLI signature: `wikihistory diff <topic> --wiki-a <url> --wiki-b <url> [--wiki-c <url> ...]`
- Existing 2-way diff behavior preserved (backward compatible)
- Bump: min 3 wikis shows column-dominant divergence; 2 wikis shows pairwise as before

## Acceptance
- [ ] 2-way diff still works
- [ ] 3-way diff produces a 3-column table
- [ ] Outlier detection flags divergent wikis
- [ ] Gate: build, lint, typecheck, test
